title: CIL Civil — Full-length Mock 05
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: As per Income Tax Department data (June 2026), India's net direct tax collections in early FY27 rose by about what percentage year-on-year?
questions.solution: Net direct tax collections rose ~14.6% YoY to about Rs 5.21 trillion.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, which country became the first Central Asian member of the New Development Bank (NDB)?
questions.solution: Uzbekistan became the first Central Asian nation to join the BRICS-backed NDB.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which India-born theoretical physicist won the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics?
questions.solution: Rajasthan-born physicist Jainendra K. Jain received the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution ranked first in the QS World University Rankings 2027 (released June 2026)?
questions.solution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ranked first in QS World University Rankings 2027.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In June 2026, which Indian jurist was elected a Judge of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)?
questions.solution: Indian jurist Bimal N. Patel was elected to ITLOS in June 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, PM Narendra Modi received the highest national award of which country?
questions.solution: PM Modi was conferred Slovakia's highest national award in 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026, in which an Indian Army contingent participated, was held in which country?
questions.solution: The multinational Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026 was held in Mongolia.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was named the new Chairman of GIC Re in June 2026?
questions.solution: The government named Hitesh Joshi as the new Chairman of GIC Re.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, ONGC was assigned to develop a new strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) facility at which location?
questions.solution: ONGC was tasked with building a new underground crude oil SPR cavern at Mangaluru, Karnataka.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The India–UK CETA and Social Security Agreement were reported to take effect from which date?
questions.solution: Reports indicated the India–UK CETA would take effect from July 15, 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The most abundant metal in the Earth's crust is:
questions.solution: Aluminium is the most abundant metal in the Earth's crust.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who chaired the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution?
questions.solution: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee of the Constitution.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was the first President of India?
questions.solution: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of India (1950–62).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: How many Fundamental Rights are currently guaranteed by the Indian Constitution?
questions.solution: There are six Fundamental Rights after the Right to Property was removed in 1978.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Directive Principles of State Policy are enshrined in which Part of the Constitution?
questions.solution: Directive Principles are contained in Part IV (Articles 36–51).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who is the constitutional head of the Indian Union?
questions.solution: The President of India is the constitutional head of the Union.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the national animal of India?
questions.solution: The Royal Bengal Tiger is the national animal of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the national bird of India?
questions.solution: The Indian Peafowl (peacock) is the national bird of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the national flower of India?
questions.solution: The lotus is the national flower of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the longest river in India?
questions.solution: The Ganga is the longest river flowing within India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the highest mountain peak entirely within India?
questions.solution: Kangchenjunga is the highest peak located within India's borders.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Tropic of Cancer passes through how many Indian states?
questions.solution: The Tropic of Cancer passes through eight Indian states.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the currency of Japan?
questions.solution: The Yen is the currency of Japan; Yuan is Chinese, Won is Korean.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the currency of the United Kingdom?
questions.solution: The UK's currency is the Pound Sterling (£).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Where are the headquarters of the United Nations located?
questions.solution: The UN headquarters is located in New York City, USA.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two trains 140 m and 130 m long move in opposite directions at 54 km/h and 48 km/h. The time to cross each other is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Opposite directions add speeds; convert km/h to m/s with the factor 5/18, then divide the combined length by the relative speed.
**Formula.** $t=\tfrac{L_1+L_2}{(s_1+s_2)\times\frac{5}{18}}$
**Given.** L₁ = 140 m, L₂ = 130 m, s₁ = 54 km/h, s₂ = 48 km/h.
**Working.** Relative $=(54+48)\times\tfrac{5}{18}=28.33$ m/s; $t=\tfrac{140+130}{28.33}$.
**Answer.** $t=9.53$ s.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A trader marks goods 50% above cost, then gives a 25% discount. His net profit percentage is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net selling factor is the markup factor times the discount factor; profit% is that factor reduced by 1.
**Formula.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{m}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{d}{100}\right)$
**Given.** Markup m = 50%, discount d = 25%.
**Working.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{50}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{25}{100}\right)=1.13$.
**Answer.** Profit $=(f-1)\times100=12.5\%$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A vessel holds 40 L of milk. 20 L is drawn out and replaced with water; this is repeated 3 times. The milk remaining is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each draw-and-replace keeps the fraction $(1-x/V)$ of milk; after n repeats this fraction is raised to the n-th power.
**Formula.** $M=V\left(1-\tfrac{x}{V}\right)^{n}$
**Given.** V = 40 L, x = 20 L, n = 3.
**Working.** $=40\left(1-\tfrac{20}{40}\right)^{3}$.
**Answer.** $M\approx 5$ L.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 7:10 is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The hour hand sweeps 30° per hour and 0.5° per minute, the minute hand 6° per minute; take the smaller of the two angles.
**Formula.** $\theta=\left|30H-5.5M\right|$
**Given.** H = 7, M = 10.
**Working.** $=\left|30\times7-5.5\times10\right|=155^\circ$; smaller $=\min(155,360-155)$.
**Answer.** $\theta=155^\circ$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The difference between compound and simple interest on Rs 8000 at 12% per annum for 2 years is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Over 2 years the gap between compound and simple interest is the first year's interest-on-interest.
**Formula.** $CI-SI=P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$
**Given.** P = Rs 8000, r = 12% p.a., t = 2 yr.
**Working.** $=8000\left(\tfrac{12}{100}\right)^2$.
**Answer.** $CI-SI=$ Rs 115.2.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A invests Rs 5000 for 8 months and B invests Rs 6000 for 4 months. Out of a total profit of Rs 4500, A's share is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit is split in the ratio of capital × time invested by each partner.
**Formula.** $\text{share}_A=P\cdot\tfrac{x\,m_A}{x\,m_A+y\,m_B}$
**Given.** A: Rs 5000×8 mo; B: Rs 6000×4 mo; profit Rs 4500.
**Working.** Ratio $=40000:24000$; share$_A=4500\times\tfrac{40000}{64000}$.
**Answer.** A's share $=$ Rs 2813.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A and B invest in the ratio 4 : 6. Out of a total profit of Rs 12000, A's share is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The compound ratio of 4 : 3 and 4 : 5 is:
questions.solution: Compound ratio = (4×4) : (3×5) = 16 : 15.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is 18% of 360?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A number divided by 7 gives quotient 12 and remainder 4. The number is:
questions.solution: Number = divisor × quotient + remainder = 7×12+4 = 88.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A car covers a distance at 60 km/h in 5 hours. The distance is:
questions.solution: Distance = speed × time = 60 × 5 = 300 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Successive discounts of 20% and 25% on a marked price of Rs 2000 give a final price of:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An item bought for Rs 600 is sold for Rs 672. The profit percentage is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: 3 kg of rice at Rs 30/kg is mixed with 5 kg at Rs 50/kg. The average price per kg is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The average age of 5 persons is 25 years. If a person aged 33 leaves, the average of the remaining 4 is:
questions.solution: Total = 125; remaining = 125 − 33 = 92; average = 23.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 10 men complete a work in 18 days, 5 men will complete it in:
questions.solution: Men-days constant: 10×18 = 5×D, D = 36 days.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A 150 m long train crosses a pole in 12 seconds. Its speed is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Pipe A fills a tank in 6 h and pipe B in 5 h. Together they fill it in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Compound interest on Rs 20000 at 10% per annum for 2 years is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An article sold at Rs 704 incurs a 12% loss. Its cost price is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is the average of the first 20 natural numbers?
questions.solution: Average of first n naturals = (n+1)/2 = (20+1)/2 = 10.5.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 60% of a number is 90, the number is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The HCF of 40 and 56 is:
questions.solution: The highest common factor of 40 and 56 is 8.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The probability of getting an even number on a single throw of a fair die is:
questions.solution: Count the favourable outcomes out of 6 equally likely faces to get 1/2.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 63 is divided in the ratio 2 : 5, the larger part is:
questions.solution: Larger part = 63 × 5/7 = 45.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'FAD'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of F, A, D to get 11.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All pens are books. All books are tables. Which conclusion necessarily follows?
questions.solution: Applying the rules of categorical syllogism, the valid conclusion is: All pens are tables.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term in the series: 5, 11, 23, 47, 95, ?
questions.solution: Each term = previous × 2 + 1, so the next term = 95 × 2 + 1 = 191.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 6 km towards north, then turns east and walks 8 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(6² + 8²) = √100 = 10 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: X is the husband of Y. Z is the mother of Y. How is Z related to X?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Mother-in-law.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A=1, B=2, ... then the sum of the letters of CAB is:
questions.solution: Add the alphabet positions of C, A, B to get 6.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: 3 : 9 :: 5 : ? (pattern n : n^2)

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution: The only daughter of her mother is the woman herself, so she is the man's mother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 5 km north, then 12 km east. His distance from the start is:
questions.solution: Resultant = √(5² + 12²) = 13 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A person facing north turns 90° to the right. Now they face:
questions.solution: Turning right from north leads to East.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 16, 25, 36, 49, ?
questions.solution: These are perfect squares; next = 8² = 64.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 3, 5, 9, 11?
questions.solution: 3, 5, 11 are prime; 9 is composite.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 3, 6, 12, 24, ?
questions.solution: Each term is multiplied by 2, so next = 24 × 2 = 48.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: Cow, Goat, Tiger, Sheep?
questions.solution: Tiger belongs to a different category from the rest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: AB, DE, GH, ?
questions.solution: Each pair skips one letter; after GH comes JK.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.solution: Each number is halved, so 24 → 12.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If each letter is replaced by the next letter, STAR is coded as:
questions.solution: Shift each letter forward by one to get TUBS.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 2 January is a Tuesday, what day is 9 January?
questions.solution: 9 − 2 = 7 days, exactly one week, so it is again Tuesday.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 20 people, X is 5th from the left. X's position from the right is:
questions.solution: Position from right = 20 − 5 + 1 = 16.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Hand : Glove :: Foot : ?
questions.solution: Just as Glove relates to Hand, Sock relates to Foot.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 64, 125, 200, 216?
questions.solution: The others are perfect cubes; 200 is not.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: Y, W, U, S, ?
questions.solution: Letters decrease by 2 each time, so the next letter is Q.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 3, 6, 12, 21, ?
questions.solution: Differences grow by 3; adding the next difference gives 33.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which of these fractions is the largest?
questions.solution: Comparing decimal values, 4/5 is the largest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: All roses are flowers. All flowers fade. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution: Both premises are universal affirmatives, so the chained conclusion 'All roses fade' follows.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Victory'.
questions.solution: 'Victory' is opposite to 'defeat'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Expand'.
questions.solution: 'Expand' is opposite to 'contract'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Generous'.
questions.solution: 'Generous' is opposite to 'stingy' (mean with money).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Transparent'.
questions.solution: 'Transparent' (see-through) is opposite to 'opaque'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Optimist'.
questions.solution: An optimist's opposite is a 'pessimist'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.solution: The correct spelling is 'Occasion' (one c, two s).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'She is good ___ mathematics.'
questions.solution: The idiom is 'good at' a subject or skill.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He has been living here ___ 2010.'
questions.solution: 'Since' is used with a point of time like a year.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Divide this ___ two parts.'
questions.solution: 'Divide into' is the correct collocation for separation.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He is married ___ a doctor.'
questions.solution: The correct collocation is 'married to' someone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The book is ___ the table.'
questions.solution: 'On' indicates resting on a surface.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a person who cannot read or write':
questions.solution: Such a person is 'illiterate'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The study of living organisms is called:
questions.solution: 'Biology' is the study of all living organisms.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One who studies stars and celestial bodies is an:
questions.solution: An 'astronomer' scientifically studies celestial bodies.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a speech made without preparation':
questions.solution: An unprepared speech is delivered 'extempore'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: A place where birds are kept is called:
questions.solution: An 'aviary' houses birds; an apiary keeps bees.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to break the ice' means:
questions.solution: 'Break the ice' means to initiate conversation in a tense or new setting.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'a piece of cake' means:
questions.solution: 'A piece of cake' means something very easy to do.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'once in a blue moon' means:
questions.solution: 'Once in a blue moon' means something happening very rarely.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to bury the hatchet' means:
questions.solution: 'Bury the hatchet' means to end a quarrel and make peace.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to let the cat out of the bag' means:
questions.solution: It means to accidentally reveal a secret.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The plural of 'Child' is:
questions.solution: 'Child' has the irregular plural 'children'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The plural of 'Mouse' is:
questions.solution: 'Mouse' has the irregular plural 'mice'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Neither he nor I ___ wrong.'
questions.solution: With 'neither...nor', the verb agrees with the nearer subject 'I', so 'am'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Each of the students ___ a book.'
questions.solution: 'Each' is singular, so it takes 'has'.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The combined correction for curvature and refraction in levelling (d in km) is about:
questions.solution: Curvature (−0.0785 d²) and refraction (+0.0112 d²) combine to about −0.0673 d² m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The hydraulic mean depth of a circular pipe running full of diameter D is:
questions.solution: For a full circular pipe, hydraulic radius R = A/P = D/4.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An axial load of 80 kN acts on a bar of cross-sectional area 1000 mm². The direct stress is:
questions.solution: Stress = P/A = 80000 N / 1000 mm² = 80 MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Terzaghi's bearing capacity factor Nc for φ = 0 (strip footing) is:
questions.solution: For φ = 0, Terzaghi's Nc = 5.7.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Rankine active earth pressure coefficient Ka for a soil with φ = 30° is:
questions.solution: Ka = (1−sinφ)/(1+sinφ) = 0.33 for φ = 30°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shear strength of a cohesionless soil is governed by:
questions.solution: For sands, shear strength = σ' tanφ (no cohesion).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The characteristic compressive strength of M20 concrete is:
questions.solution: M20 denotes a characteristic cube strength of 20 MPa at 28 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the fore bearing of a line is 60°, its back bearing is:
questions.solution: Back bearing = fore bearing ± 180° = 240°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most economical rolled section for a steel beam in bending is the:
questions.solution: The I-section concentrates material in the flanges, giving high section modulus per unit weight.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If E = 120 GPa and Poisson's ratio = 0.25, the modulus of rigidity G is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: First-order BOD kinetics means the rate of oxidation is proportional to the:
questions.solution: BOD follows first-order kinetics, so the rate is proportional to the organic matter still remaining.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a perfect plane truss, the relation between members m and joints j is:
questions.solution: A statically determinate (perfect) plane truss satisfies m = 2j − 3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever 3 m long carries a 5 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W × L = 5 × 3 = 15 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The degree of static indeterminacy of a simply supported beam is:
questions.solution: A simply supported beam is statically determinate (indeterminacy = 0).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 80 MPa, σy = 60 MPa and τxy = 20 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 80 MPa, σy = 60 MPa, τxy = 20 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=70$, radius $=22.36$; $\sigma_1=70+22.36$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=92.36$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The modular ratio m in working stress design is taken as:
questions.solution: IS 456 gives the modular ratio as m = 280/(3σcbc).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The development length Ld of a bar is given by:
questions.solution: Ld = φσs/(4τbd), where φ is bar diameter and τbd is design bond stress.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 5 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 5 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a cantilever produces its maximum moment at the fixed support.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{2}$
**Given.** w = 5 kN/m, L = 5 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{5\times5^2}{2}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=62.5$ kNm at the fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ratio of maximum to average shear stress for a rectangular section is:
questions.solution: For a rectangle, maximum shear stress is 1.5 times the average.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The cross-sectional area of one 20 mm diameter bar is approximately:
questions.solution: Area = π/4 × 20² = 314 mm².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The specific gravity of soil solids is typically about:
questions.solution: The specific gravity of most soil solids is around 2.65.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a well-graded soil, the coefficient of curvature Cc lies between:
questions.solution: Well-graded soils have a high uniformity coefficient and Cc between 1 and 3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The velocity head corresponding to a velocity of 3 m/s is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A point of contraflexure in a beam is where:
questions.solution: At a point of contraflexure the bending moment is zero and reverses sign.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 4 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 10 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a cantilever produces its maximum moment at the fixed support.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{2}$
**Given.** w = 10 kN/m, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times4^2}{2}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=80$ kNm at the fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slenderness ratio of a column is defined as:
questions.solution: Slenderness ratio = effective length / least radius of gyration.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The carry-over factor for a prismatic member in moment distribution is:
questions.solution: For a prismatic member with a far fixed end the carry-over factor is 1/2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superelevation for a design speed of 60 km/h and radius 300 m (e = V²/127R) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shear stress distribution across a rectangular beam section is:
questions.solution: Transverse shear stress varies parabolically, peaking at the neutral axis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 400 mm, clear cover 40 mm and 16 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Compression steel in a doubly reinforced beam is provided when:
options: Mu exceeds the limiting moment Mu,lim
questions.solution: When the design moment exceeds the section's balanced (limiting) capacity, compression steel is added.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The classical bearing capacity theory for shallow footings was given by:
questions.solution: Terzaghi's bearing capacity theory is the standard for shallow foundations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The detention time of a tank of volume 100 m³ treating a flow of 50 m³/h is:
questions.solution: Detention time = Volume / Discharge = 100 / 50 = 2 hours.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The degree of static indeterminacy of a propped cantilever beam is:
questions.solution: A propped cantilever has one redundant reaction, so indeterminacy = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular channel 5 m wide carries 6 m³/s. The critical depth (yc = (q²/g)^{1/3}) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Critical depth in a rectangular channel depends only on the discharge per unit width q.
**Formula.** $y_c=\left(\tfrac{q^2}{g}\right)^{1/3}$
**Given.** Q = 6 m³/s, B = 5 m, g = 9.81 m/s².
**Working.** $q=\tfrac{6}{5}=1.2$ m²/s; $y_c=\left(\tfrac{1.2^2}{9.81}\right)^{1/3}$.
**Answer.** $y_c\approx 0.53$ m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape of the ideal transition curve used on highways is a:
questions.solution: An ideal transition curve is a spiral where radius varies inversely with length.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Müller-Breslau principle is used to draw:
questions.solution: It states the influence line for a response equals the deflected shape from a unit displacement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 10 kPa and friction angle φ = 25°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 120 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 10 kPa, φ = 25°, σ = 120 kPa.
**Working.** $=10+120\times\tan 25^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=65.96$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A hydraulic jump in an open channel forms when the flow changes from:
questions.solution: A hydraulic jump dissipates energy as the flow shifts from supercritical to subcritical.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge through a section of area 1.5 m² with velocity 3 m/s is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sum of distribution factors at a rigid joint equals:
questions.solution: Distribution factors at any joint always sum to unity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a balanced section with Fe415 steel, the limiting neutral axis depth factor xu,max/d is:
questions.solution: IS 456 specifies xu,max/d = 0.48 for Fe415 steel.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed beam carrying a UDL w over span L, the support (fixed-end) moment is:
questions.solution: Fixed-end moment for a UDL is wL²/12 at each support.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The relation between porosity n and void ratio e is:
questions.solution: By definition, n = e/(1+e).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 5 m carries a central point load of 8 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM = WL/4 = 8 × 5 / 4 = 10 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Extra widening of a carriageway on a horizontal curve accounts for:
options: Off-tracking of rear wheels plus a psychological allowance
questions.solution: Extra widening covers mechanical off-tracking and a psychological width allowance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Castigliano's first theorem gives:
questions.solution: The partial derivative of strain energy with respect to a load gives the deflection at that load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 100 MPa, σy = 20 MPa and τxy = 20 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 100 MPa, σy = 20 MPa, τxy = 20 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=60$, radius $=44.72$; $\sigma_1=60+44.72$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=104.72$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The limit state method of design checks both:
questions.solution: Limit state design verifies the limit states of collapse (strength) and serviceability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 8 m carries a central point load of 30 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 30 kN, L = 8 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{30\times8}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=60$ kNm at mid-span.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 165" width="320" height="165" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="40" y1="55" x2="280" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="3"/><polygon points="40,55 31,72 49,72" fill="#64748b"/><polygon points="280,55 271,72 289,72" fill="#64748b"/><line x1="160" y1="16" x2="160" y2="46" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><polygon points="160,53 154,42 166,42" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="160" y="12" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">W = 30 kN</text><text x="160" y="88" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">span L = 8 m</text><polygon points="40,112 160,144 280,112" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.12)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="160" y="159" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">BMD — Mmax = 60 kNm</text></svg>
figure.caption: Simply supported beam — central point load

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The method best suited for analysing trusses with a single redundant member is the:
questions.solution: Consistent deformation (unit load) handles internally redundant trusses efficiently.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Darcy's law states that the discharge velocity v equals:
questions.solution: Darcy's law: v = ki, where k is permeability and i is hydraulic gradient.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The thermal stress developed in a fully restrained bar for a temperature rise ΔT is:
questions.solution: Restrained thermal stress = αEΔT, independent of length and area.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Euler's critical buckling load of a column is proportional to:
questions.solution: Pcr = π²EI/Le², so it varies inversely with the square of effective length.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M20 concrete, width 300 mm, effective depth 450 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 20 MPa, b = 300 mm, d = 450 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times20\times300\times450^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 167.7$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The characteristic yield strength of Fe415 reinforcing steel is:
questions.solution: Fe415 denotes a yield strength of 415 MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an open channel, the specific energy is minimum at the:
questions.solution: The specific energy curve attains its minimum value at the critical depth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge over a rectangular weir (Cd = 0.62) of length 2 m under a head of 0.5 m is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Discharge over a sharp-crested rectangular weir varies with the 3/2 power of the head.
**Formula.** $Q=\tfrac23 C_d\sqrt{2g}\,L\,H^{3/2}$
**Given.** C_d = 0.62, L = 2 m, H = 0.5 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac23\times0.62\times\sqrt{2\times9.81}\times2\times0.5^{1.5}$.
**Answer.** $Q\approx 1.29$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The phenomenon of sudden reduction in compressive strength of a slender column is called:
questions.solution: Slender compression members fail by elastic buckling before yielding.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-ended column (E = 200 GPa, I = 10×10⁻⁶ m⁴, length 3 m) has an Euler buckling load of about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A pin-ended column buckles at the Euler critical load with effective length equal to the actual length.
**Formula.** $P_{cr}=\tfrac{\pi^2 E I}{L^2}$
**Given.** E = 200 GPa, I = 10×10⁻⁶ m⁴, L = 3 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{\pi^2\times200\times10^9\times10\times10^{-6}}{3^2}$ N.
**Answer.** $P_{cr}\approx 2193.2$ kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The moment distribution method was developed by:
questions.solution: Hardy Cross developed the moment distribution method in 1930.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lateral ties in an RCC column primarily prevent:
questions.solution: Ties hold the main bars in position and prevent their lateral buckling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a void ratio e = 0.75, the porosity n is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Terzaghi's one-dimensional theory deals with the:
questions.solution: Terzaghi's theory describes one-dimensional consolidation of saturated soils.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 10 kPa and friction angle φ = 20°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 100 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 10 kPa, φ = 20°, σ = 100 kPa.
**Working.** $=10+100\times\tan 20^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=46.4$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 800, the maximum slenderness ratio for a load-bearing steel compression member is:
questions.solution: The limiting slenderness ratio for members carrying loads is 180.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The partial safety factor for yielding of steel (γm0) in IS 800 limit state design is:
questions.solution: IS 800:2007 specifies γm0 = 1.10 for yielding.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 5 m carries two equal 20 kN point loads placed 2 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 20 kN, a = 2 m, span = 5 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=20$ kN; $M_{max}=20\times2$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=40$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The strain energy stored per unit volume under axial stress σ is:
questions.solution: Resilience (strain energy density) = σ²/2E.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section modulus of a rectangular section 200 mm wide and 400 mm deep is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A quick sand condition occurs when the effective stress becomes:
questions.solution: When upward seepage reduces effective stress to zero, the soil loses strength (quick condition).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The cement compound that liberates the greatest heat of hydration is:
questions.solution: C3A hydrates fastest and releases the most heat of hydration.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 140 MPa and 40 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 140 MPa, σ₃ = 40 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{140-40}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=50$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A three-hinged arch is:
questions.solution: Three equilibrium equations plus the hinge condition make it determinate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 5 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 5 kN/m, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{5\times4^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=10$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The plasticity index of a soil is:
questions.solution: Plasticity Index = Liquid Limit − Plastic Limit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Torsional shear stress in a circular shaft is given by:
questions.solution: The torsion formula is τ/r = T/J = Gθ/L.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 5 m clay layer (Cc = 0.4, e₀ = 1) under an initial effective stress of 100 kPa receives a 60 kPa increment. The primary consolidation settlement is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Primary consolidation of a normally consolidated clay follows the logarithmic compression law.
**Formula.** $S_c=\tfrac{C_c H}{1+e_0}\log_{10}\tfrac{\sigma_0+\Delta\sigma}{\sigma_0}$
**Given.** C_c = 0.4, H = 5 m, e₀ = 1, σ₀ = 100 kPa, Δσ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.4\times5000}{1+1}\log_{10}\tfrac{100+60}{100}$ mm.
**Answer.** $S_c\approx 204.1$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The error due to the curvature of the earth in levelling is:
questions.solution: Earth's curvature makes distant points read lower; correction is +0.0785 d² m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 30 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 30 kN, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{30\times4}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=30$ kNm at mid-span.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 165" width="320" height="165" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="40" y1="55" x2="280" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="3"/><polygon points="40,55 31,72 49,72" fill="#64748b"/><polygon points="280,55 271,72 289,72" fill="#64748b"/><line x1="160" y1="16" x2="160" y2="46" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><polygon points="160,53 154,42 166,42" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="160" y="12" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">W = 30 kN</text><text x="160" y="88" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">span L = 4 m</text><polygon points="40,112 160,144 280,112" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.12)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="160" y="159" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">BMD — Mmax = 30 kNm</text></svg>
figure.caption: Simply supported beam — central point load

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Standard Proctor test is used to determine:
options: Optimum moisture content and maximum dry density
questions.solution: The Proctor compaction test gives the OMC and maximum dry unit weight.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bolts subjected to both shear and tension are checked for:
questions.solution: An interaction (combined) check is required when bolts carry shear and tension together.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 60 MPa and 20 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 60 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{60+20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=40$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456, the minimum grade of concrete for reinforced concrete work is:
questions.solution: IS 456 specifies M20 as the minimum grade for RCC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 80 MPa and 20 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 80 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{80+20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=50$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Standard Penetration Test (SPT) gives the soil's:
questions.solution: The SPT reports the blow count N used to estimate relative density and strength.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456, the maximum spacing of main bars in a slab is the smaller of:
questions.solution: Maximum spacing of main reinforcement is 3 times effective depth or 300 mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RL of a benchmark is 95.5 m. With back sight 1.2 m and fore sight 0.8 m, the RL of the next point is:
questions.solution: RL = 95.5 + 1.2 − 0.8 = 95.9 m (rise and fall).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The flexure (bending) equation is:
questions.solution: The bending equation relates moment, stress and curvature as M/I = σ/y = E/R.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The velocity head corresponding to a velocity of 4 m/s is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A weld used to join two overlapping plates is a:
questions.solution: Fillet welds are used at the edges of overlapping or lapped plates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Soil with particle size less than 0.002 mm is classified as:
questions.solution: Particles finer than 0.002 mm are classified as clay.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a simply supported beam with a central point load of 24 kN, the maximum shear force is:
questions.solution: Each reaction = W/2 = 12 kN, which is the maximum shear force.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kinematic indeterminacy of a structure equals the number of:
questions.solution: Kinematic indeterminacy = number of independent unknown joint displacements (degrees of freedom).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If LL = 40% and PL = 25%, the plasticity index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lug angles in a steel tension member are provided to:
options: Reduce the length of the end connection
questions.solution: Lug angles add connecting area so the gusset connection length is shortened.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The partial safety factor for steel in the limit state method (IS 456) is:
questions.solution: γm for reinforcing steel is 1.15 in limit state design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The pressure at a depth of 5 m in water (ρ = 1000 kg/m³, g = 9.81 m/s²) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The partial safety factor for concrete in the limit state method (IS 456) is:
questions.solution: γm for concrete is 1.5 in limit state design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed beam with a central point load W over span L, the magnitude of the fixed-end moment is:
questions.solution: Fixed-end moment for a central point load is WL/8 at each support.
